Contact Rare & Manuscript Collections

Dr. Joyce Brothers papers, 1955-2006.
Collection Number: 4254

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Dr. Joyce Brothers papers, 1955-2006.
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
4254
Abstract:
Papers of Dr. Joyce Brothers include correspondence, subject files, letters received, scripts, clippings, contracts, awards, photographs, cartoons, slides, and articles and books by Dr. Brothers. Audiovisual Archives of Dr. Joyce Brothers include 463 motion picture films, ca. 796 video tapes, and 184 audio recordings.
Creator:
Brothers, Joyce.
Quanitities:
33.3 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE

Psychologist.
Cornell University Class of 1947, Columbia University Ph.D. 1955.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Papers of Dr. Joyce Brothers include correspondence, subject files, letters received, scripts, clippings, contracts, awards, photographs, cartoons, slides, and articles and books by Dr. Brothers. Audiovisual Archives of Dr. Joyce Brothers include 463 motion picture films, ca. 796 video tapes, and 184 audio recordings.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Dr. Joyce Brothers papers, #4254. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Collection is closed for processing.
SUBJECTS

Subjects:
Psychologists -- United States.
Popular culture.
Celebrities.
Form and Genre Terms:
Audiotapes.
Videotapes.
Motion pictures (visual works).
CONTAINER LIST
Container
Description
Date
Series. Consult Dr. Brothers
Scope and Contents
Consult Dr. Brothers changed titles and is also referred to as "The Dr. Joyce Brothers Show" or "The Dr. Joyce Brothers Program" in the early years. It is unclear when exactly the name changed, but was clearly Consult Dr. Brothers in 1961. Documents that were not labelled with the show title were labelled as "Consult Dr. Brothers" for consistency. The show was picked up by NBC and later ABC (1961). It ran from 1959 to circa 1961 and preceded "Tell Me, Dr. Brothers". 65 episodes. In 1961, letters say "ready to shoot" 1-28, so maybe Dr. JB Show was pre-61, and Consult started with ABC in 1961? but referred to in publicity pamphlets in 1962 as "The Dr. Joyce Brothers show"
Subseries a. Files
Box 39 Folder 82
Consult Dr. Brothers show topics
 1960-1962
Box 39 Folder 83
Dr. Joyce Brothers Program budget
 1962-10
Box 41 Folder 1
Notes and correspondence
 1961-1963
Scope and Contents
These notes likely relate to Consult Dr. Brothers, as they were found with related material, but may also contain information on "Tell Me, Dr. Brothers." Included are notes for redaction and censorship for the show from WEWS.
Box 41 Folder 2
Notes and correspondence
 1961-1965
Scope and Contents
These notes likely relate to Consult Dr. Brothers, as they were found with related material, but may also contain information on "Tell Me, Dr. Brothers."
Box 41 Folder 3
Consult Dr. Brothers publicity
 Undated
Scope and Contents
Includes publicity and publicity booklet from ABC Films.
Box 41 Folder 4
Meet Dr. Brothers publicity for "Dr. Joyce Brothers," WRCA-TV
 1958-07

Video 12618 V-12618
Consult Dr. Brothers promos
 1961-03-24-1961-03-25
Scope and Contents
Duplicate of the promos on V-12617, for channels 2-12, and general promo, filmed on March 25 ,1961. Followed by 30 minutes of blank tape, then 10 second promos for channels 2-12, and general promo. These promos were filmed on March 24, 1961.

Consult Dr. Brothers
Scope and Contents
Includes commercials. Topics: my second wife resents things I did with my first wife; husband critical of wife's shopping.

This series contains numbered articles and segments, arranged by Brothers. She referred to them "Shorties."

Series . Correspondence
Subseries a. Letters from the public
Scope and Contents
This subseries contains letters from viewers, listeners, readers, and fans. The bulk of this correspondence is advice letters. For related correspondence, see individual series for Brothers' shows and publications.

Series . Television and Radio Programs
Subseries a. "Dr. Joyce Brothers' Casebook"
Scope and Contents
Proposed program where Dr. Brothers dramatic presentation of cases based on actual letter. This show was 20-25 minutes. Brothers was represented by Lester Lewis Associates. This is a different show than Consult Dr. Brothers/The Dr. Joyce Brothers show. It may not have been filmed or aired.
Box 40 Folder 1
Contract for "Dr. Joyce Brothers Casebook" writers
 1960-02-18
Scope and Contents
Folder contains an unsigned contract between writer Stephen Gethers and Brothers (Lester Lewis Associates). Signed contracts with Adrian Spies, Steven Gethers
Box 39 Folder 5
Budget correspondence and documentation
 1960
Scope and Contents
Payment and employment documentation for script writers, costs for program
Box 39 Folder 6
Cost sheets and documentation
 1960
Box 39 Folder 14-15
General documents
 1958-1960

Notes, budgets, correspondence, script on frigidity and other untitled scripts, notes, and an employee handbook.

Series. Research Files
Subseries a. Card files
Scope and Contents
Known as the "Tobies" (after Brothers' assistant), this card file consists of index cards with articles stapled to them. When asked to speak or right on a specific subject, Brothers' would pull from these cards as her references.
